R Notebook

library(brms)
library(tidyverse)
library(BayesFactor)
library(BayesianId)
data("penguins")
test_data = na.omit(penguins) %>% dplyr::rename(y = sex, x1 = flipper_len, x2 = bill_len)

fit = lapreg(y ~ x1 + x2, data = test_data, family = "bernoulli_logistic", prior_scale = 100) # large prior scale -> results should be similar to MLE
compare = glm(y ~ x1 + x2, data = prepare_data(parse_formula(y ~ x1 + x2), test_data), family = binomial())
fit$optim_fit$value
[1] -218.3161
# perhaps it's "bernoulli_logit_glm_lpmf" that's messing things up by dropping some constants
# no, it's the Hessian matrix being verrueckt
fit$log_evidence
[1] -225.9893
attr(,"logarithm")
[1] TRUE
solve(-fit$optim_fit$hessian) |> round(5)
            (Intercept)       x1       x2
(Intercept)     0.01366  0.00000  0.00002
x1              0.00000  0.00012 -0.00021
x2              0.00002 -0.00021  0.00087
solve(-fit$optim_fit$hessian) |> det() |> log()
[1] -20.85993
determinant(fit$optim_fit$hessian, logarithm = TRUE)$modulus[1]
[1] 20.85995
det(fit$optim_fit$hessian)
[1] -1146437810
333*log(0.5)
[1] -230.818
logLik(compare)
'log Lik.' -209.9689 (df=3)
plot(Barsoom)

plot(bar)

library(BayesFactor)
library(dplyr)
n = 8
mu_a = 10
mu_b = 13
sigma = 1

df = data.frame(group = c("a", "b") |> rep(each = n),
                y = c(rnorm(n = n, mean = mu_a, sd = sigma),
                      rnorm(n = n, mean = mu_b, sd = sigma))
                )

t.test(y ~ group, data = df, var.equal = TRUE)

    Two Sample t-test

data:  y by group
t = -5.0244, df = 14, p-value = 0.0001859
alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group a and group b is not equal to 0
95 percent confidence interval:
 -4.612189 -1.852567
sample estimates:
mean in group a mean in group b 
       9.500422       12.732800 
ttestBF(formula = y ~ group, data = df)
Bayes factor analysis
--------------
[1] Alt., r=0.707 : 113.2834 ±0%

Against denominator:
  Null, mu1-mu2 = 0 
---
Bayes factor type: BFindepSample, JZS
1/ttestBF(formula = y ~ group, data = df)
Bayes factor analysis
--------------
[1] Null, mu1-mu2=0 : 0.008827418 ±0%

Against denominator:
  Alternative, r = 0.707106781186548, mu =/= 0 
---
Bayes factor type: BFindepSample, JZS
